text: The robotic low anterior resection begins with six ports positioned for splenic flexure mobilization. The surgeon approaches from medial to lateral, clearing fibrolymphatic structures around the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA). The ureter is visible behind the mesocolon, and pelvic nerves are identified. The IMA is secured and transected. The Gerota's fascia is pushed down, revealing the pancreas bulge. The transverse mesocolon over the pancreas is transected, and the inferior mesenteric vein is secured.

The surgeon completes the splenic flexure mobilization by dissecting the gastrocolic omentum and the splenic flexure itself. The robotic arms two and three are repositioned, and the pelvic dissection proceeds. The ureter, pelvic nerve, and gonadal vessels are visible, along with the ongoing mesorectal dissection.

The surgeon performs anterior dissection of the rectum, encountering severe adhesions due to the patient's previous abdominal hysterectomy. The posterior aspect of the dissection is shown, with the pelvic floor clearly visualized.

The surgeon performs anterior dissection, clearing fibro-fatty tissue from the posterior vaginal wall, revealing its glistening white layer. The rectum is fully mobilized and transected using a green stapler, followed by an anastomosis between the rectal stump and the colon.
